frameworks.name: language-instructed deep RNN
frameworks.one_line: recurrent neural network receives pretrained language embeddings to perform novel tasks

frameworks.name: compositional generalization
frameworks.one_line: shared geometry between language and sensorimotor activity enables zero-shot task composition

frameworks.name: pretrained language model embeddings
frameworks.one_line: NLP-derived instruction representations scaffold task-relevant neural codes

core_question: How can a network of neurons interpret linguistic instructions to perform novel tasks without explicit task experience?
key_assumptions: linguistic instructions can be embedded via a pretrained language model
sensorimotor task representations can be structured by language
shared geometry between semantic and motor codes supports generalization
manipulations: presence vs absence of linguistic instructions
training on a set of common psychophysical tasks
communication between partner models via generated descriptions
key_findings: best models perform unseen tasks at 83% correct from instructions alone
language scaffolds sensorimotor activity into a geometry shared with semantic embeddings
model can generate a linguistic description of a novel task from motor feedback
the generated description can guide a partner model to perform the task
interpretations: language cues the proper composition of practiced skills in unseen settings
common geometry between instructions and sensorimotor activity supports compositional generalization
